mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not decline when rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline when the trigger activates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and surges in consumer price inflation indicate stagflationary pressures that erode real disposable income and corporate margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate demand that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly higher during recovery periods compared to contraction periods when GDP growth accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below 98.0 signals a contraction in manufacturing output that reduces corporate cash flows and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline when industrial production breaches the 98.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and elevated consumer price inflation compresses real yields and forces aggressive monetary tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain variance in forward returns beyond individual series effects (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarterly contraction in real final sales of domestic product indicates weakening aggregate demand that precedes earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral when real final sales contract by the specified threshold.
